@graph.name: Pressure washing
@graph.description: Exterior pressure washing and soft washing for residential and commercial properties. Includes house siding, driveways, sidewalks, decks, fences, roofs (soft wash), and commercial storefronts. We bring water and equipment.

mainEntity.name: How much does pressure washing a house cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pressure washing a single-story house typically costs $150 to $400. A two-story house ranges $300 to $700. Driveway washing alone ranges $80 to $200. Pricing depends on square footage, height, accessibility, and how much mildew or algae buildup is present.

mainEntity.name: Is pressure washing safe for roof shingles?

acceptedAnswer.text: High-pressure washing is not safe for shingle roofs and can void roof warranties. Roofs require soft washing: low pressure (under 500 PSI) combined with a sodium hypochlorite or non-chlorine biocide solution. This kills mold, algae, and lichen without damaging shingle granules.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between pressure washing and soft washing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pressure washing uses 2,000 to 4,000 PSI of water to mechanically remove dirt from durable surfaces (concrete, brick, fence). Soft washing uses under 500 PSI plus a cleaning solution to chemically remove biological growth from delicate surfaces (roof shingles, painted siding, screened enclosures). Most reputable services do both; the surface determines the method.

mainEntity.name: Do I need to be home during pressure washing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not usually for exterior work. We need access to one outdoor water spigot (if not bringing our own tank) and a clear work area. For driveway or sidewalk work, move vehicles before the appointment. For house washing, close windows. Payment can be invoiced after the work is done with photos.

mainEntity.name: How often should I have my house pressure washed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Once per year is standard in humid climates (Florida, Gulf Coast, southeastern US). Once every 2 years is acceptable in drier climates. Roofs benefit from a soft wash every 18 to 24 months in humid climates to prevent algae streaking. Driveways can be done annually or as needed.
